title.zh: 三代追夢：法國移民後裔的教育階梯與種族隱形壁壘
title.en: Beyond the Second Generation: How France’s Third-Generation Immigrants Are Still Confronting the Legacy of Ethnic Hierarchy

summary.zh:
法國向來以「種族平等」為國家核心價值，但這項研究揭開了三代移民後裔教育路徑的真實圖景。研究者追蹤法國移民家庭從祖父母到孫輩三代人的教育流動，發現南歐移民孫輩已與本地人趨於持平，但北非移民孫輩仍處於教育階梯底端。這就像一座高樓，南歐家族搭上電梯一路攀升，北非家族卻被困在底層，即便努力踮腳也難以觸及頂層。

研究顯示，種族階層並非全然消失——南歐移民後代證明社會能提供平等機會，但北非後代卻遭遇制度性障礙。這種差異揭示了「平等」的陰影：當不同族裔被賦予不同重量的砝碼，即便同樣奔跑，也難以追上。這不僅是個人奮鬥的問題，更是社會結構深植的種族偏見。
summary.en:
France’s national narrative of ethnic equality has long been a cornerstone of its identity, but this study reveals that third-generation immigrants still navigate a deeply stratified educational landscape. By tracking intergenerational mobility over three generations, researchers found that grandchildren of Southern European immigrants achieve parity with native French families, suggesting equal opportunities. In contrast, the grandchildren of North African immigrants face enduring educational disadvantages, indicating a persistent ethnoracial hierarchy.

This research underscores that while some immigrant groups have overcome historical barriers, systemic inequalities remain for others. The findings challenge simplistic narratives of integration, showing that "equality" is not a monolithic concept but varies by ethnoracial background. For North African descendants, the shadow of discrimination lingers, revealing the need for targeted policies beyond assimilation rhetoric.

keyArticle.title: Shades of Égalité : Educational Mobility and Ethnoracial Hierarchy Over Three Generations in France
